channel.description: AP News 首页抓取 + 英译简

item.title: Trump threatens Iran with more strikes but says recent fighting does not mean a return to war
item.description:
DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump threatened Wednesday to unleash more military strikes against Iran after saying that Iranian attacks signaled the end of the ceasefire. But he later said the most recent exchange of fire with the Islamic Republic did not herald a return to full-scale war.
Trump, who isknown for sending mixed messagesabout his intentions, has repeatedly threatened to escalate strikes against Iran, only to pull back after claiming diplomatic progress. He said Wednesday that the U.S. would “probably hit them hard again tonight,” and later added that the latest back-and-forth fighting would not result in “long-term” military action.
“Anything that happens is going to happen very fast,” Trump said, though he also suggested the U.S. military might “just finish the job.”
A day after assaults on commercial shippingescalated into an exchange of strikeson Iranian and U.S. military targets, Trump also renewedhis past threatsto hit Iran’s civilian infrastructure, including electric plants and desalinization plants, and to seize theoil-production hub of Kharg Island.
Speaking on the sidelines ofa NATO summitin Ankara, Turkey, Trump said the strikes are continued retaliation for Iranian attacks on commercial shipsin the Strait of Hormuz.
“They are behaving very badly,” he said of Iran, accusing the country of launching drones and a missile at ships. After three tankers were hit Tuesday, the U.S. launched strikes on Iran, and Iranian forces retaliated by attacking American military sites in the Persian Gulf.
U.S. President Donald Trump meets with Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on the sidelines of the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, Wednesday, July 8, 2026. (AP Photo/Alex Brandon)
Iran has asserted that the interim ceasefire deal gives it the right to manage traffic through the strait. Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf, a key negotiator in talks seeking a permanent end to the war, was defiant in a post on X: “The era of bullying and extortion is over. It leads nowhere. We don’t fold.”
The U.S. military has attacked Iran after it said Tehran struck three ships in the Strait of Hormuz. Jon Gambrell with The Associated Press in Dubai explains how the assault Wednesday came as part of an American effort that also revoked the Islamic Republic’s ability to openly sell crude oil in the world market.
The latest exchange of fire raised fears that thewar in Irancould reignite, and Trump fueled those concerns by saying the interim agreement to pause fighting was “over,” although he added that he would allow negotiations to continue.
Attacks have repeatedly threatened the shaky ceasefire, but Trump’s comments added new uncertainty, and oil pricesshot upafter he spoke. A renewed conflict could engulf the wider Middle East and would likely again halt energy shipments through the strait that are crucial to the global economy.
“For me, I think it’s over,” Trump said when asked about the status of the ceasefire. He added that U.S. representatives can continue negotiations, but he cast doubt on the outcome. “They can talk, but I think they’re wasting their time,” he said.
Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi, also a top negotiator, retorted on X that Trump’s remarks “are not a sign of power but an admission of the failure” of U.S. policy toward Iran.
Trump has threatened to seize Kharg Island at previous points in the war,including last month, when he also questioned whether the U.S. “has the stomach for it.” Some 90% of Iranian oil exports pass through the island.
The new attacks on ships in the strait, despite the negotiations, could reflect a divide among Iran’s leadership. Hard-liners seek lasting control over the waterway, which is a globally important conduit for fuel shipments and has become a critical lever in confronting the West. Pragmatists want a permanent peace deal to lift international sanctions and providedesperately neededeconomic relief.
Negotiations to reach a final deal had been due to start after the dayslong funeral for Iran’sSupreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed Feb. 28 in the war’s first moments. The funeral, which ends Thursday, was supposed to be a period of lower tensions.
The talks are meant to focus on the toughest matters, includingfully reopening the straitand rolling backTehran’s disputed nuclear program.
A group of people stands in shallow water as a cargo ship appears anchored in the Strait of Hormuz off Bandar Abbas, Iran, Tuesday, June 30, 2026. (Amirhosein Khorgooi/ISNA via AP)
The U.S. military’s Central Command said American forces launched strikes “to impose heavy costs for targeting and attacking commercial shipping crewed by innocent civilians in an international waterway.”
The military said it hit Iranian targets including air-defense systems, radars and over 60 small boats used by Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard.
Those boats have been key to threatening ships in the strait, through which a fifth of the world’s traded oil and natural gas passed before the war. Iran’s ability to bring shipping in the waterway to a near halt during the war proved its greatest strategic advantage.
Rising prices for energy supplies, fertilizer and food put pressure on the U.S. to make a deal.
Iranian state media reported explosions in several locations, including in Bandar Mahshahr, where a Revolutionary Guard member was killed. State television said eight members of the Army’s air and naval forces were killed in Bandar Abbas and Bushehr; the latter is home to Iran’s nuclear power plant complex.
On Wednesday morning, both Bahrain, home to the U.S. Navy’s 5th Fleet, and Kuwait, home to U.S. Army forces, sounded missile alerts. The Revolutionary Guard issued a statement acknowledging targeting U.S. military installations in both countries.
Kuwait said it intercepted two ballistic missiles and 13 drones launched by Iran. The Kuwaiti Electricity Ministry said a number of lines were out of service after shrapnel fell on them.
Women talk in front of a banner with graphic depicting the late Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei kissing head of the late commander of Iran’s Revolutionary Guard expeditionary Quds Force, Gen. Qassem Soleimani, who was killed in a U.S. drone attack in 2020 in Iraq, as they wait for the green light to cross an intersection in downtown Tehran, Iran, Monday, June 29, 2026. (AP Photo/Vahid Salemi)
After the Iranian strikes on shipping. the U.S. revoked a license that — for the first time in years — had allowed Iran to conduct oil sales openly in U.S. dollars, as part of the interim deal.
Iran and the United States agreedas part of the interim dealto allow ships to pass through the strait without paying charges for 60 days. But Tehran has insisted it must control the vessels’ routes and vowed to later charge fees for passage. That would upend decades of practice in the waterway. The ships attacked Tuesday all appeared to be using a route close to Oman’s shore, rather than one ordered by Tehran.
The U.S. and many Gulf Arab statessay they will not agree to Iran charging for passage through the strait.
Mourners gather in prayer during the funeral procession for the late Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei inside the Imam Ali Shrine in Najaf, Iraq, Wednesday, July 8, 2026. (AP Photo/Anmar Khalil)
Elsewhere, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, Iraqi Prime Minister Ali Falah al-Zaidi and other Iranian and Iraqi officials attended funeral ceremonies for Khamenei on Wednesdayin the Iraqi city of Najaf.
Khamenei’s body will be returned to Iran to be buried Thursday at the Imam Reza shrine in Mashhad, his birthplace.
Kim reported from Ankara, Turkey. Associated Press writers Nasser Karimi in Tehran, Iran; Qassim Abdul-Zahra in Najaf, Iraq; and Collin Binkley in Washington contributed.

item.title: 特朗普称美国将给予乌克兰生产爱国者防御系统的许可证
item.description:
ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump said at a NATO summit Wednesday in Turkey that the U.S. will give Ukraine a license to makePatriot air defense systemsto counter missile attacks from Russia in theirmore than four-year war, a huge coup for Kyiv which has long requested the technology.
Allowing foreign manufacture of Patriots, which the U.S. had resisted, was a turnaround for Trump that mirrored his day at the NATO meeting: Upon arriving, he lashed out at European partners for resisting his efforts totake control of Greenlandand for not supporting his war in Iran. But by day’s end, he described a gathering of unity and “tremendous love,” and praised member nations on their progress in increasing their defense spending.
NATO’s European members plus Canada have scrambled to meet the alliance’s increaseddefense spending targets, which Trump has demanded as the U.S. draws down troops in Europe and insists the continent take more responsibility for its own security.
Trump had reopened old wounds among the32 NATO leadersby insisting again ahead of the summit that the U.S. should control Greenland,a semiautonomous Danish territory. That led Denmark’s Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen to say her country is “ready to defend every inch of NATO including our own territory.”
President Donald Trump has strong words for Iran, while at the NATO summit in Turkey. AP correspondent Donna Warder reports.
Trump also blasted some European countries for refusing to participate in the Iran campaign,singling out Spainas “a terrible partner in NATO” andrenewing his threatsto cut off trade.
But the tone of Trump’s meeting with the Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy was a break from earlier encounters whichended in acrimony, and Trump praised the Ukrainian leader’s willingness to reach a deal on ending the fighting in Ukraine.
“We’ve actually developed a good relationship. It’s hard to believe,” Trump said during a news conference with Zelenskyy, adding that he believed a deal on ending the war was on the horizon and that the U.S. would “work on some kind of security package” to provide to Ukraine.
Trump said the Ukrainian president has “done an amazing job” and “been very effective” in the war as he pledged to grant Ukraine a license to manufacture the Patriot defense systems.
“We’ll give them the right to make Patriots. We’ll show them how to do it,” Trump said. “I think they can produce them pretty quickly.”
Patriots are expensive, in high demand and take a long time to produce. Zelenskyy has for years been asking for more of them, and more recently for a license so that Ukraine can manufacture its own.
Ahead of the summit, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte praised Trump for theseries of U.S. strikes on Iranovernight, after Tehran struck three merchant shipsin the Strait of Hormuz.
“I think what you did last night was absolutely necessary,” Rutte said to Trump. “It was a very strong response, and I’m with you on this.”
The U.S. strikes, as well as the revoking of a license allowing Iran to sell its oil on global markets, underscored the fragility of an interim deal to end months of fighting.
Trump said of the interim agreement with Iran: “For me, I think it’s over” — but added he will allow talks to continue.
“It’s just a waste of time dealing with them,” he said.
Rutte has dedicated ahuge amount of energyto keeping Trump’s support for NATO and to holding the summit together. On Wednesday, he sought to tamp down the president’s ire by giving him credit for recent increases in defense spending from NATO allies.
“Grab the win. It’s there,” Rutte told Trump on Wednesday.
The NATO chief pointed to countries including Estonia, Latvia, Poland and Denmark that are investing more in defense, but noted that the Trump administration expects “the Europeans and Canadians will equalize their spending with the United States.”
Last month Rutte went to Washington to hail the “Trump Trillion” — the $1.2 trillion that European allies and Canada have added to defense spending since Trump came to power in 2017.
As leaders converged on Ankara, Rutte hosted a “big reveal” event to showcase the many deals planned for the increased spending — much of it to be spent on U.S. companies, creating thousands of jobs for Americans.
At last year’s summit, the allies agreed toinvest 5% of their gross domestic product on defense— 3.5% on their defense budgets and 1.5% on infrastructure so troops and equipment can move faster in times of conflict.
Yet figures released by NATO on Tuesday showed that Slovenia, Belgium, Spain and the Czech Republic have struggled to meet the alliance’s old spending target of 2% of GDP.
The Trump administration wants to see a leaner “NATO 3.0,” with Europe taking responsibility for its own security, including Ukraine, with conventional weapons while America would continue to provide its nuclear umbrella.
The Pentagon has launched asix-month review of U.S. military presencein Europe, leaving allies to seek clarity on just how deeply Trump intends to cut U.S. force numbers.
Zelenskyy made a fresh appeal Tuesday for Ukraine to be allowed to join the alliance, saying Ukrainian armed forces are highly experienced and would only boost NATO’s defense capabilities. Russia is vehemently opposed to that.
Zelenskyy has highlighted Ukraine’s adaptability and its ability to strike deep inside Russia. He said Ukraine’s armed forces are “eliminating” on average 30,000 Russian troops every month.
In a declaration following Wednesday’s summit, NATO leaders pledged to provide Ukraine with $80 billion to help meet its defense needs this year and next, noting “the long-term threat Russia poses to Euro-Atlantic security.”
Concern has been mounting among some countries with borders near Russia that Moscow might be preparing a hybrid attack — a combination of conventional warfare with tactics like cyberattacks — on the continent as Russian President Vladimir Putin struggles tosecure victory in Ukraine.
Trump also met withSyrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa, a former insurgent who led the offensive that unseated autocrat Bashar Assad in December 2024. Despite having once been an al-Qaida fighter, al-Sharaa has won Trump’s backing as he seeks to rebuild Syria and restore its shattered ties with the West.
Later, U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said in a statement that Trump has told U.S. lawmakers that the U.S. will soon remove Syria as a state sponsor of terrorism as part of a year-long normalization process with the country’s new government.
“Lifting sanctions on Syria will unlock international trade and investment, give Syria a chance to rebuild, and open up a new chapter for the Syrian people,” Rubio said.
In June 2025, Trump signed an executive order ending a number of economic sanctions before revoking the terrorism designation a few weeks later for al-Sharaa, though the designation for Syria remained.
Associated Press journalists Collin Binkley and Michelle L. Price in Washington and Farnoush Amiri at the United Nations contributed.

item.title: Telegram 上的性掠夺者网络成员试图用暗号隐藏他们的罪行
item.description:
BERLIN (AP) — They called themselves the “German driving school for experts,” but prosecutors say the true purpose of theirTelegram chatswas to brag about the women they raped and share tips about how to drug them.
In posts that sometimes included photos and videos of their attacks on unconscious victims, they referred to women as “cars,” sedatives as “fuel” and rape as “driving,” according to court documents. They called their victims “dead pigs.”
Investigators have been poring through several years’ worth of posts in roughly two dozen group chats on the popular messaging app that authorities believe served an online predator network of mainlyChinese mentargeting mostly Chinese women inGermany. Their investigation has already led to the convictions of three alleged inner circle members on rape and other charges. A fourth was convicted Wednesday in Berlin.
The defendant covering his face in the court during a trial against an online predator network of men accused of using chat groups to share tips on how to drug and rape women, in Berlin, Germany, Wednesday, July 8, 2026. (AP Photo/Ebrahim Noroozi)
EDITOR’S NOTE: This story includes discussion of sexual violence. If you or someone you know needs help, please call 1-800-656-4673 in the U.S., 116 016 in Germany or 15117905157 in China.
“The perpetrators were characterized by a particular ruthlessness, an objectification of the victims, and the perfidious planning of their crimes,” Frankfurt chief prosecutor Dominik Mies told The Associated Press.
Major details of the investigation remain unknown, at least to the public, including how many attacks and perpetrators have been linked to the GermanTelegram chatsand how the chats, some of which reportedly had tens of thousands of members, could have operated for so long. It’s also unclear if the chats are linked to a ballooning investigation in Europe and the Americas into drug-facilitated sexual assaults by misogynist online communities.
The defendant covers his face as he arrives for the trial in Munich, Germany, Feb. 2, 2026. (Malin Wunderlich/dpa via AP)
Under German privacy laws, prosecutors are limited in what they can say outside the courtroom, documents are restricted and, in the ongoing case in Berlin, members of the public have been forced to leave the courtroom during parts of the trial.
This may be why the investigation into the Telegram group has garnered less attention in Germany than might be expected. But members of the country’s Chinese community, mostly women, have been attending court proceedings to show support for the victims even if they don’t know them.
People walk in the court on the day of the trial against an online predator network of men accused of using chat groups to share tips on how to drug and rape women, in Berlin, Germany, Wednesday, July 8, 2026. (AP Photo/Ebrahim Noroozi)
“What makes one really angry is to see that such groups hate women, they have no respect,” said Fu Xiao, who traveled roughly 500 kilometers (310 miles) to Berlin last week to attend the trial. “Women aren’t seen as people.”
In China, state media has covered the cases comprehensively, but wider discussion about the prosecutions on Chinese-language social media like Rednote has beenpartially censored. Certain tags have been more likely to get a post deleted or banned on Rednote, screenshots and searches show. But posts using less direct language havesurvived the censors, including ones that refer to “date rape” or the euphemistic “students studying abroad in Germany.”
China’s Ministry of Public Security and Rednote didn’t respond to requests for comment.
The German cases have drawn comparisons to the attacks onGisèle Pelicot, a French woman who, over the course of nearly a decade,was repeatedly drugged and rapedby her then-husband and strangers he invited to their home. The trial — and Pelicot’s decision to waive her anonymity — prompted a reckoning over rape culture in France and beyond.
“Pelicot is not an isolated case,” Judge Markus Koppenleitner said during a hearing in Munich for one of the Chinese men convicted in the German investigation. “This is not a Chinese or French phenomenon, but one that also exists in Germany and, ultimately, worldwide.”
The defendant covers his face as he arrives for the trial, in Munich, Germany, Feb. 2, 2026. (Malin Wunderlich/dpa via AP)
Similar cases to the “German driving school” investigation have been popping up around the globe. Although authorities haven’t publicly linked them to the German prosecutions, some investigators have cited tips from German authorities and journalists as crucial to their progress.
In Los Angeles, German investigators last year reached out to police about a potential suspect in drug-facilitated sexual assaults. The defendant, a graduate student from China, is accused of drugging and sexually assaulting three women in LA after he allegedly procured the drugs from a Chinese national in Germany.
In the Netherlandslast month, police arrested four men suspected of drugging and sexually abusing women after hearing from authorities in Germany and the U.K. Dutch police said the alleged perpetrators used social media chat groups to disseminate videos showing the abuse and discuss how to drug victims.
And Europol, the European Union’s police agency, last week announced “Project Medusa,” an international operation designed to dismantle online networks that promote drug-facilitated sexual assaults. Law enforcement from Germany and the U.K. are leading the operation, which has already netted 57 arrests.
The German predator network managed to thrive despite clear violations of Telegram’s terms of service, again raising questions about how the platform has been used forcriminal activity.
In 2024, the app’s founderwas arrested in Parisover allegations that the platform was being used for illicit activity, including drug trafficking and the distribution of child sexual abuse images. He denied wrongdoing, blaming surging numbers of Telegram users that he said “caused growing pains that made it easier for criminals to abuse our platform.” The investigation is ongoing.
“Sexual violence is explicitly forbidden by Telegram’s terms of service and such content is routinely removed,” the company said in a statement. “Telegram fulfils all of its legal obligations in relation to such harmful content, including everything set out by” the European Union’s Digital Services Act.
The company didn’t respond to questions about the German cases, including how photos, videos and comments about sexual crimes were posted for years in the app, whether Telegram was aware of the activity and what, if anything, it did to alert the authorities.
The defendant covering his face in the court during a trial against an online predator network of men accused of using chat groups to share tips on how to drug and rape women, in Berlin, Germany, Wednesday, July 8, 2026. (AP Photo/Ebrahim Noroozi)
Some of the German Telegram chats date back to at least 2020, court documents show. Attorney Magdalena Gebhard, who represented a victim in a previous Berlin trial that led to a conviction, said there was an inner circle of eight perpetrators but that some of the chat groups had up to 50,000 members.
Police only became aware of the network in 2024 after a man in Frankfurt, referred to by German courts as Dapeng Z., changed his tactics from drugging and sexually abusing female acquaintances to targeting strangers he met online, according to prosecutors.
German police arrested Dapeng Z., whom German and Chinese media have reported is the group’s ringleader, in 2024 in cooperation with Chinese law enforcement, according to the Chinese consulate in Frankfurt and the Beijing News, a state-run media outlet.
He was sentenced in February to 14 years in prison for aggravated rape, attempted murder and other offenses, though he has appealed. His attorneys didn’t respond to a request for comment.
Although authorities haven’t publicly said how many women were victimized by the “driving school” network, they have said their investigation is ongoing, meaning there could be further arrests and additional victims. Gebhard’s client, for example, only learned she had been sexually assaulted after investigators discovered video footage.
The defendant covering his face in the court during a trial against an online predator network of men accused of using chat groups to share tips on how to drug and rape women, in Berlin, Germany, Wednesday, July 8, 2026. (AP Photo/Ebrahim Noroozi)
On Wednesday, Zhiting S., a 32-year-old trained medic, was convicted of being an accessory to rape, among other charges, and sentenced to five years in prison. The defense plans to appeal the verdict.
The Berlin state court found that in the chats, Zhiting S. had pointed to a particular sedative before an assault by the man convicted in Frankfurt, though he wasn’t alone in offering such advice.
Zhiting S. also was convicted on three charges of sexual coercion related to alleged abuse of his partner in China. Video recordings led investigators to those crimes.
Defense attorney Ehssan Khazaeli said earlier that his client has admitted being part of a chat group but did not offer any significant advice.
Wu reported from Bangkok. Associated Press reporters Geir Moulson and Fanny Brodersen in Berlin, Molly Quell and Mike Corder in The Hague, Netherlands, Jaimie Ding in Los Angeles and Jill Lawless in London contributed to this report.

item.title: 曼哈顿高层建筑在柱子弯曲后被认为稳定，部分疏散措施已解除
item.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — An under-construction Manhattan high-rise at risk of collapse was stabilized late Tuesday and some evacuations of nearby buildings were lifted.
“We’ve been monitoring the building for many hours and have not seen any movement,” Ahmed Tigani, commissioner of the New York City Department of Buildings, said during a news conference Tuesday.
By nighttime, residents were allowed back into several of the seven buildings that were evacuated as a precaution.
The scene unfolded after columns were spotted buckling on Tuesday morning at the 1970s-era building, which is being converted into luxury apartments. Construction workers at the site and people in nearby buildings — including a school, diplomatic offices and several hotels — in the busy corridor of midtown were rushed out after firefighters were called there around 8 a.m. MayorZohran Mamdanidescribed it as “an extremely serious situation.”
City officials going floor-by-floor later found no additional movement of the damaged columns, giving on-site contractors the greenlight to move forward with emergency repairs, his office said. On Tuesday evening, workers could be seen shoring up the damage inside the gleaming glass-and-steel high rise.
A firetruck is parked at the intersection of 3rd Avenue and East 42nd Street after buildings in the area were evacuated, Tuesday, July 7, 2026, in New York. (AP Photo/Angelina Katsanis)
That work is expected to continue, impacting a part of Manhattan near the famed Grand Central train station that is a hub for metro area commuters and residents as well as tourists.
The building, which is theformer headquartersof pharmaceutical giant Pfizer, is located just down the street from New York City icons like the Chrysler Building and the United Nations headquarters.
Leila Bozorg, one of Mamdani’s deputy mayors, said it was “encouraging” the building did not appear to be shifting as officials went up into and past the damaged floors on their way to the 37th floor — the top floor — of the building.
From the street below, a badly bent structural column could be seen through a large glass window on the 21st floor. The fire department, which alsoposted imagesof the column, said they found multiple cracks and sagging floors as well.
Asked earlier in the day if there was concern of a collapse, Fire Chief John Esposito said the way the steel-framed building is constructed, “it would not be a total collapse, it would be more of a localized collapse.”
Nearby buildings and streets remained evacuated for much of the day, including a school and the Israeli consulate just across the street. The former Pfizer building itself was empty at the time, other than for the construction workers.
Ramesh Yallappa, a tourist who was among those evacuated from a nearby hotel, said he initially feared it was a fire in the hotel when an immediate evacuation was ordered Tuesday morning.
“That moment, we were really really scared,” he said.
With more than 1,600 units, the developers say the project is thelargest office-to-residential conversionin the city’s history. Gensler, the architectural firm leading the project, says on its website that it is transforming a pair of 1970s-era office buildings by adding more than a dozen stories and redesigning an adjoining tower.
Building department records show the project has been fined by the city for several safety violations, including glass and metal falling off the building, along with an incident where a worker fell off a ladder.
Spokespersons for Gensler and MetroLoft, the project developer, didn’t return messages seeking comment.
But in a statement to The New York Times, MetroLoft stressed that the building itself is not at risk of collapse and that no debris fell from the building.
Nathan Berman, founder of MetroLoft,told The Wall Street Journalthat the added weight from widening the top 15 or so floors of the building likely caused the damage. The two columns that buckled may not have been properly reinforced, he told the newspaper.
“Why those particular two columns and nothing else? We don’t know,” Berman told the Journal. “We’re investigating that.”
He maintained the building’s integrity wasn’t compromised.
“Ninety-five percent of the building, the structure is sound and intact,” Berman told the Journal. “There is no way that this corner of a small extension all of a sudden topples this building.”
Emily Guglielmo, a structural engineer based in California, said the buckled columns are likely not repairable and will need to be removed and replaced.
“A lot of these things — cracking, deflections, sagging — those elements are probably not salvageable,” she said.
Replacing the columns will require rigorous analysis, and the repairs will be expensive, experts said.
The short-term solution is shoring up the structure and the floors, said Abi Aghayere, a professor of structural engineering at Drexel University in Philadelphia.
Shoring involves installing four-legged scaffolding to temporarily carry the load that the structure is supposed to carry until the columns can be replaced, Aghayere said.
Yi Bao, associate professor of civil engineering at the Stevens Institute of Technology in New Jersey, said the building could be damaged beyond the buckled columns, which could have forced the loads to be redistributed to different parts of the building.
Ed Miller, an area resident, said he walks under the building’s scaffolding several times a day, but will find other routes home going forward.
“The building was pretty old,” said Miles Grant, who said he used to work at the building. “It definitely needed a lot of work to become ready for residential.”
This story has been updated to correct that city officials revised the building’s height to 37 stories, not 38.
Izaguirre reported from Lindenhurst, New York. Associated Press reporters David R. Martin in New York, Jessica Hill in Las Vegas and Hallie Golden in Seattle contributed to this story.

item.title: 威斯康星州前法官因阻碍 ICE 逮捕墨西哥移民而免于入狱
item.description:
密尔沃基（美联社）——周三，前威斯康星州法官汉娜·杜甘（Hannah Dugan）因在美国移民和海关执法局人员试图在法院走廊逮捕一名墨西哥被告时将一名墨西哥被告从陪审团室门口带入监狱而被保释。
美国地区法官林恩·阿德尔曼 (Lynn Adelman) 对她处以 5,000 美元罚款，称此案是一个原本善良的人对这个国家的移民政策感到不满，却在当下做出了错误的决定。
67 岁的杜根于 12 月被判犯有妨碍司法罪重罪。她的律师在审判期间辩称，唐纳德·特朗普总统的政府试图“镇压”杜根，以确保司法部门遵守 ICE 针对移民出席法庭听证会的战略。
杜根于一月份辞去了担任了九年的密尔沃基县巡回法官一职，因为共和党州议员威胁要弹劾她，称她为激进法官。她在辞职信中表示，她的起诉威胁到“我们司法机构的独立性”。美国共和党众议员汤姆·蒂芙尼（Tom Tiffany）是特朗普的忠实拥护者，竞选威斯康星州州长，他曾敦促当局“将她关起来”。
马凯特大学的两名法学教授——一名前州最高法院法官和一名耶稣会牧师——代表她发言，称杜根是受压迫人民的捍卫者，他们不应该受到惩罚。“汉娜树立了基督徒的榜样，”牧师格雷戈里·奥米拉 (Gregory O’Meara) 说。
杜根在法庭上发表讲话，称她作为一名法官已尽力而为，她在 2025 年 4 月那天的行为并非恶意，而是为了维护“法庭的礼仪和安全”。
杜根说：“我被塑造成既是罪犯又是英雄。我两者都不是。我是一名公务员，只是想做好我的工作。”她补充说，由于她和她的家人受到威胁，她不得不退出公共生活。
美国检察官执行助理理查德·弗罗林承认杜根经历了附带后果，但表示“法官不能选择无视法律”。
法官表示，杜根失去了工作，现在被判重罪，并受到威胁，迫使她搬家并停止参​​加社区活动。他还指出，杜根的行为并没有阻止 ICE 特工在法院外逮捕被告。
阿德尔曼表示：“这一定罪证明没有人可以凌驾于法律之上。”
检察官在量刑备忘录中辩称，杜根违反了她作为法官的誓言，并将执法部门和公众置于危险之中。
“法官被赋予极大的自由裁量权，但有一条他们不能跨越的界限，”弗罗林写道。“被告越过了那条线。”
杜根的律师辩称，她已经“受到足够的惩罚”，不应被判处超出她在联邦拘留期间的任何监禁时间。
联邦量刑指南要求监禁 15 至 21 个月，但这位由比尔·克林顿总统于 1997 年任命的法官不受这些指南的约束。检察官没有建议判处刑罚，但弗罗林写道，“这是一项严重的罪行，应该判处相应的严重刑罚。”
杰森·卢扎克律师在宣判后表示，他们仍会对杜根的妨碍司法重罪定罪提出上诉。陪审员在审判中宣判她无罪，罪名是隐匿他人以防止被捕，属于轻罪。
2025 年 4 月 18 日，移民官员在得知 31 岁的爱德华多·弗洛雷斯-鲁伊斯 (Eduardo Flores-Ruiz) 非法入境美国并计划在杜根 (Dugan) 出席州殴打案听证会后前往密尔沃基县法院。
杜根在法庭外与特工对峙，并指示他们前往首席法官办公室，称他们的行政令不足以逮捕弗洛雷斯-鲁伊斯。她的律师表示，在审判期间，她遵守了要求法院工作人员向其主管报告任何移民代理的协议。
特工离开后，她带领弗洛雷斯-鲁伊斯和他的律师走出私人陪审团大门。特工在走廊里发现了弗洛雷斯-鲁伊斯，跟踪他到外面，并在一番追逐后逮捕了他。一周后，联邦调查局特工在法院逮捕了杜根，并给她戴上手铐。
美联社撰稿人包括爱荷华州得梅因的 Hannah Fingerhut。鲍尔从威斯康星州麦迪逊报道。

item.title: 当麦康奈尔与共和党领导人交谈时，人们对住院的麦康奈尔的健康状况产生了各种猜测
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——据助手称，参议院两名共和党领袖已单独与肯塔基州参议员米奇·麦康奈尔进行了交谈，这位前共和党领导人因未公开的健康问题入院三周多后仍在医院。
麦康奈尔的助手拒绝透露有关他病情的任何信息，这引发了人们对他的预后以及参议院在两周休会后下周返回华盛顿时他是否足够健康以出席国会大厦的猜测。84 岁的麦康奈尔将于一月份任期结束时退休。
参议院多数党领袖约翰·图恩（R-S.D.）的发言人表示，他周一与麦康奈尔通了电话，两人进行了“漫长而实质性的对话，涵盖了包括国家安全在内的各种话题”。作为领导人，图恩通常会随时了解会议的最新疾病和缺席情况，因为他必须处理计票结果和他以 53 比 47 的微弱多数优势。
据发言人称，怀俄明州参议员、参议院共和党二号人物约翰·巴拉索周二与麦康奈尔进行了 20 分钟的交谈。声明称，两人讨论了中期选举前的参议院竞选、最高法院和其他话题。
“麦康奈尔参议员全身心投入，并渴望回到参议院，”巴拉索发言人凯特·诺伊斯说。
麦康奈尔的另一位盟友、共和党策略师斯科特·詹宁斯(Scott Jennings)在X上发帖称，他周二也与麦康奈尔进行了20分钟的交谈，并且“他仍在医院康复中”。詹宁斯说，他们谈论了政治、外交政策，“甚至还谈了一点参议院的历史”。
根据麦康奈尔办公室的一份声明，麦康奈尔于 6 月 14 日入院，仅表示他“正在接受优质的护理”。
一周后的一份声明称，他那周不会投票。周四，一份新声明称，他“感谢在医院继续康复期间所获得的大量支持。”
声明称：“参议员不断进步，并在参议院休会期间与他的工作人员就肯塔基州和参议院事务密切合作。”
麦康奈尔的发言人周二再次发表了同样的声明，但没有新的更新。
这位参议员近年来多次住院治疗后出现了未具体说明的健康问题。
当麦康奈尔仍担任共和党领袖时，他于 2023 年 3 月因脑震荡入院，并在华盛顿一家酒店摔倒后错过了数周的工作。回国后的新闻发布会上，他两次僵住了，茫然地盯着前方，直到同事和工作人员——包括身为医生的巴拉索——前来帮助他。
一年后，他在走出共和党午餐会时摔倒并扭伤了手腕。
麦康奈尔在童年时期就患有小儿麻痹症，他很早就承认成年后走路和爬楼梯有些困难。2019年，他还在肯塔基州的家中绊倒，并接受了肩部骨折的手术。
这位肯塔基州参议员于 1984 年首次当选参议院议员，从 2007 年到去年一直是共和党领袖，在此期间担任多数党和少数党领袖。作为一名普通参议员，他一直很活跃，在众议院开会时出席工作，经常使用轮椅出行。

item.title: 随着要求普拉特纳退出缅因州参议员竞选的呼声越来越高，继任之争已经开始
item.description:
PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Democratic nominee Graham Platner hasn’t resigned from the Maine U.S. Senate race following an allegation of sexual assault. But the succession battle to take his place was already underway Tuesday.
The allegation reported Monday prompted a chorus of calls for Platner to depart the high-stakes race against Republican Sen. Susan Collins, which coulddecide party control of the Senate. Platner’s long-time backer, Sen. Bernie Sanders, “recommended that he step aside.” Then, Tuesday evening,The Washington Postreported that an ex-girlfriend of Platner’s had accused him of removing condoms during sex without her consent.
Platner, who has denied all the allegations, has been mum on whether he’ll step aside. And the Maine Democratic Party, charged with creating a process to pick his replacement, hasn’t publicly announced their plans.
Democratic U.S. Senate candidate Graham Platner speaks during a primary election night watch party after winning the Democratic nomination Tuesday, June 9, 2026, in Blue Hill, Maine. (AP Photo/Robert F. Bukaty)
On Tuesday night, Executive Director Devon Murphy-Anderson released a video saying the party is developing an “open, inclusive, transparent and fair” process but won’t disclose details until Platner withdraws. Platner’s team has repeatedly tried to “put their thumb on the scale,” she said.
“We have repeatedly reiterated to Graham Platner’s team that they have no role in determining our next Democratic nominee for the U.S. Senate, nor in determining what this process looks like,” she said.
An official for the Platner campaign responded that it “has reached out to the party to try and understand what this process would look like” and “at no point has the campaign tried to ‘put its finger on the scale.’” The official added that thousands of Maine residents voted and volunteered for Platner and they should play a role in the decision.
“While Graham wouldn’t want to be a part of the process, he would want to make sure the voters and volunteers make this decision — not the political establishment,” the statement said.
So far, the succession battle has played out in a murky power vacuum. A growing number of candidates are teasing their entry into the race as a fight shapes up between the progressive wing and the establishment camp of the Democratic Party.
Platner became a populist hero among the party’s left flank, pulling far ahead of an establishment-backed candidate, Gov. Janet Mills, in the primary. Now, progressive groups fear that political ground could be lost.
“To the Democratic establishment: This is not your opening,” said Joseph Geevarghese, who leads Our Revolution. The organization founded by Sanders backed and then withdrew its endorsement of Platner after the sexual assault allegation. The group said Tuesday that it was “rallying behind” another progressive contender, Troy Jackson, who announced he’s “exploring” a candidacy.
In the allegation published Monday by Politico, a woman whom Platner previously dated said he drunkenly forced her to have sex after she told him to stop. It is the latest in astring of controversiesthe first-time candidate had weathered, but the seriousness of the assault claim was too far for many of his supporters in the Democratic Party.
Jenny Racicot, who lives in Maine, told Politico that Platner entered her home in 2021 while drunk and assaulted her. Racicot said she had been in an on-and-off relationship with Platner, but she cut off contact with him after that night and told him the incident wasn’t consensual. She said in a CNN interview on Monday evening that she opted not to fight back for fear of Platner, a former Marine, becoming more violent.
U.S. Sen. Bernie Sanders, I-VT., speaks during a Get Out The Vote rally ahead of New York’s primary election, Thursday, June 18, 2026, in the Brooklyn borough of New York. (AP Photo/Ryan Murphy)
The pressure for Platner to withdraw from the Senate race has only increased given the short deadlines Maine law allows for replacing general election candidates. There is no mechanism for Democrats to remove Platner from the ballot, and the deadline to withdraw is 5 p.m. July 13. State law gives the authority to choose a replacement to the state party. Any replacement candidate must be named by July 27.
The lack of communication from Platner and the state’s Democratic Party has contributed to some confusion about what comes next as candidates tentatively toss their hats in the ring.
Some are arguing that the next Democrat should echo Platner’s progressive messaging, pointing to his success at rallying voters across the state. Others cautioned that having ties to Platner will only doom an already uphill campaign against Collins. Many are pushing for an open process even with limited time.
“No one’s enjoying this experience, and it seems like the consensus step forward should be having a fair and open process that everybody feels is legitimate,” said Adam Green, co-founder of the Progressive Change Campaign Committee, a political action committee that backed Platner but now calls for him to suspend his campaign.
One possible contender, Nirav Shah, the former director of Maine’s Center for Disease Control and Prevention, said Tuesday he was “evaluating” whether to join the race. Shah said he’s been in contact with the Maine Democratic Party about ensuring a possible replacement process is based on “openness, transparency and robustness.”
“Every single day that we don’t have a nominee, and a process and a clear pathway for the nominee, is another day that we’re letting Sen. Collins continue to get her message out,” Shah told The Associated Press. “As of right now, it’s not clear what the process will be.”
Shah, who came in second in this year’s Maine Democratic gubernatorial primary, added, “I want to give the party grace, because we’re building this plane as we’re flying it.”
Maine’s governor, Mills, who sought the Democratic Senate nomination but dropped out beforethe June 9 primary, could be considered as another contender. Mills was supported by Senate Democratic leader Chuck Schumer but abandoned her campaign, saying she couldn’t raise the money needed to compete.
Another possible replacement is Jackson, Maine’s former state Senate President, who unsuccessfully ran to be the Democratic gubernatorial nominee earlier this year with the backing of Platner and Sanders. Jackson filed paperwork with the Federal Election Commission Tuesday to launch a Senate exploratory committee.
“Remember: Progressives overwhelmingly won the primary. A progressive MUST be on the ballot,” Our Revolution said in a fundraising email, which described Jackson as a logger and union leader who helmed Sanders’ past presidential efforts in Maine.
Jordan Wood, a former U.S. Senate candidate who then switched to unsuccessfully run for Maine’s 2nd District, posted Tuesday that he was “continuing conversations” with voters about joining the race.
Other names circulating include Shenna Bellows, the current Maine Secretary of State; Dan Kleban, founder of Maine Beer Co.; and Hannah Pingree, currently Maine’s Democratic gubernatorial nominee.
Bellows said in a statement she will “seriously consider entering this race.”
Joanie Monteith, a passionate supporter from the southern Maine town of York who organizeda trivia night about Platnerin March, said through tears Tuesday that she was devastated by the news. She was waiting for another public statement from Platner before making a decision about whether she could keep supporting him.
“I’m numb, and I’m waiting for what Graham has to say,” she said. “I’m trying not to be a part of this public trial. And I’m heartbroken. And I’m heartbroken for him and his wife.”
She added that she believes the allegations are serious.
“I’m not going to blame a victim. Because if this is true I feel very bad for the woman,” she said.
Another Maine voter, Lee Holman, said she wants Platner to stay in the race.
“I feel like the people of Maine have spoken,” the Democrat said. “If they wanted Janet Mills, they could have voted for her.”
She said the allegation against Platner may be legitimate, but she questions the timing. Democrats, she added, can be too quick to “throw the baby out with the bathwater” by calling on politicians facing allegations to resign.
“Every time we think we have a chance to snatch our democracy back, something gets in the way,” she said.
Democratic U.S. Senate candidate Graham Platner, right, and his wife Amy Gertner gesture to supporters during a primary election night watch party June 9, 2026, in Blue Hill, Maine. (AP Photo/Robert F. Bukaty, File)
Kruesi reported from Providence, R.I. and Bedayn from Austin, Texas. Matt Brown in Minneapolis and Ali Swenson in New York City contributed.

item.title: 两名美国足球队工作人员因比利时世界杯比赛被国际足联停职
item.description:
佛罗里达州迈阿密花园（美联社）——美国足球队的两名工作人员在周一对阵比利时的 16 强比赛中被国际足联停职。
国际足联没有解释为什么对球队经理萨姆·萨帕特卡和美国足协安全副总裁弗兰克·潘内尔施加纪律。
美国足协周二没有给出任何理由，只是表示这与成功解除前锋福拉林·巴洛贡的单场红牌停赛无关。
萨帕特卡自 2020 年起担任球队的行政经理，并自 2015 年起在 USSF 工作。
美国队以1-4负于比利时队，被淘汰出世界杯。

item.title: 国际足联正在调查世界杯比赛中球迷与主播 IShowSpeed 之间的事件
item.description:
佛罗里达州迈阿密花园（美联社）——国际足联周二表示，正在调查周五阿根廷队与佛得角队之间的世界杯比赛期间，一名主播和 YouTuber IShowSpeed 在看台上与一名球迷发生的事件。
IShowSpeed 正在直播比赛，可以听到一名穿着阿根廷球衣的球迷询问人们对他说了些什么。这位用西班牙语说话的粉丝似乎是在告诉黑人 IShowSpeed“去动物园哭吧”。
国际足联在一份声明中表示：“国际足联强烈谴责一切形式的种族主义、仇恨和歧视。”“这些行为在足球界、国际足联世界杯或社会任何地方都没有立足之地。……国际足联世界杯是对团结、多样性和尊重的庆祝。它将来自世界各地的人民、文化和社区聚集在一起，任何破坏这些价值观的人都不会出现在我们的比赛中。”
发送给 IShowSpeed 和公关人员寻求评论的消息没有立即得到回复。
IShowSpeed 是一名 21 岁的美国人，拥有超过 5000 万 YouTube 订阅者、4500 万 Instagram 粉丝和 4700 万 TikTok 粉丝。

item.title: 欧盟议员呼吁对国际足联主席就特朗普在美国与比利时比赛前的接触进行调查
item.description:
布鲁塞尔（美联社）——数十名欧洲议员正在争取支持，准备在欧洲议会对国际足联主席詹尼·因凡蒂诺展开调查，原因是他参与了允许美国前锋福拉林·巴洛贡参加比赛的决定，尽管此前他曾获得红牌。
7月1日，巴洛贡在世界杯美国队战胜波黑的比赛中被出示红牌，这通常会让他失去参加球队下一场比赛的资格，但在美国总统唐纳德·特朗普代表这位25岁的前锋与因凡蒂诺进行交涉后，国际足联于周一解除了他的禁赛资格。
欧洲议会议员巴里·安德鲁斯、拉拉·沃尔特斯和尼尔斯·福格桑在一份联合声明中表示，世界足球管理机构“在比赛中改变红牌禁赛规则的决定是一种耻辱，也是对正义的歪曲”。
声明称：“我们再次看到因凡蒂诺和国际足联屈服于特朗普政府的要求。”
议员们要求欧盟国家的国家足球协会敦促国际足联道德委员会调查因凡蒂诺以及来自特朗普政府的压力是否是解除禁赛的一个因素，以及“其他潜在违反政治中立的行为”，例如授予特朗普国际足联和平奖。
国际足联表示，解除禁赛是纪律委员会的决定。
议员们表示，迄今为止已有 35 名同事签署了这封信。
“体育运动的美妙之处在于它建立在公正和透明的规则之上。当因凡蒂诺允许政治压力来决定谁能参加比赛时，这种公平感就消失了，”他们说。

item.title: ABC fights back against FCC regulators in dispute over 'The View' and equal time rules
item.description:
美国广播公司正在继续强烈反击联邦监管机构重新审视其热门脱口秀节目《The View》是否受到平等时间规则的质疑。
该网络指责特朗普政府试图在不断升级的争端中压制言论自由，并在周二公开的向联邦通信委员会提交的一份新文件中辩称，该问题在二十多年前就已由该委员会自行解决。
作为 FCC 审查流程的一部分，需要提交最新的 ABC 备案。它包括对委员会的“回复评论”，以支持该电视网要求做出一项声明性裁决的请愿书，即《观点》——一个结合了娱乐和政治采访的长期播出的早间节目，经常以批评唐纳德·特朗普总统的评论为特色——是一个真正的新闻节目。
ABC 引用了 2002 年 FCC 的一项决定，对《The View》进行了限定，这意味着它不受同等时间规则的约束。这些规则要求给予竞争公职的候选人平等的通话时间。
在 5 月份的一份文件中，美国广播公司同样指责特朗普政府试图通过重新提出有关“观点”的问题来冷却其受宪法保护的言论自由并阻碍公开的政治讨论。
这是美国媒体与特朗普政府之间法律领域内外更广泛争议的最新一起，争议焦点是记者认为总统对言论自由和媒体履行职责的能力的攻击。特朗普一直批评那些新闻报道与他的议程背道而驰的媒体。
他的联邦通信委员会主席布伦丹·卡尔表示，他打算辩称“The View”不是所谓的真正的新闻节目。该问题可能会影响其他类似地结合娱乐和政治的节目。
在 5 月份提交文件后，FCC 就该问题开启了公众评议期，作为审查过程的一部分。ABC 在周二提交的文件中指出，公众已提交了超过 77,000 条评论，其中“不可否认的大多数”信息支持“观点”和言论自由。
新提交的文件称：“评论者的担忧是正确的。”“第一修正案不允许政府担任编辑的职位。然而，这就是委员会现在提议占据的席位……决定哪些广播节目有资格成为合法新闻，对于那些委员会认为有需要的节目，迫使他们将播出时间交给他们从未选择的嘉宾。”
报告称，关于《观点》的争议触及了一个更大的原则：“联邦监管机构是否可以推翻广播公司对采访对象的编辑判断——宪法将这一判断交给广播公司及其观众，而不是国家。”
美国广播公司还辩称，“自二十多年前委员会上次回答该问题以来，法律所关心的‘观点’没有任何变化……改变的不是该计划，而是其周围的政治气候。”
它声称卡尔的联邦通信委员会选择将注意力集中在白天和深夜的节目上，“被认为对现任政府不友好——而没有触及谈话广播的广阔前景，候选人通常会在没有对手的情况下出现。”
它说，这“不是公平的监管”。
FCC 发言人在给美联社的电子邮件中回应称 ABC 误导公众。
该发言人表示：“虽然 ABC 坚称《The View》是法律规定的‘善意新闻节目’，但 ABC 应该专注于遵守其公共利益义务，而不是误导公众。”
政府对《观点》的批评与对批评特朗普的深夜新闻主持人——尤其是美国广播公司的吉米·金梅尔的不满相呼应。唐纳德和梅拉尼娅·特朗普最近都呼吁美国广播公司解雇金梅尔，因为金梅尔在漫画中形容第一夫人“焕发着准寡妇的光芒”。基梅尔说，这个笑话是对这对夫妇年龄差异的轻微讽刺。
乔斯林·诺维克 (Jocelyn Noveck) 为美联社报道媒体和娱乐的交叉领域。

item.title: 《皮特》以 25 项艾美奖提名领跑，《黑客》以 24 项艾美奖提名打破喜剧片纪录
item.description:
洛杉矶（美联社）——周三艾美奖提名公布后，《皮特》在第二季以 25 项提名领跑所有喜剧类提名，而《黑客》在最后一季以创纪录的 24 项提名领跑所有喜剧片。
HBO Max 在剧情类和喜剧类奖项中都位居榜首，其中《黑客》打破了《工作室》去年创下的喜剧类剧集提名最多的纪录。
艾美奖投票者喜欢即将离任的节目，也喜欢自第一季以来喜剧代际紧张的《黑客》。这两种相互冲突的现象让它在五年级时的数字不断上升。明星简·斯玛特在前四季中都获得了喜剧类最佳女主角，如果她在九月份没有获得第五名，那就太令人震惊了。
她在整个剧集中的搭档汉娜·艾因宾德 (Hannah Einbinder) 去年取得了突破，并第四次获得喜剧类女配角提名，周三获得了第五个提名，她的搭档、该剧的联合创始人保罗·W·唐斯 (Paul W. Downs) 也获得了第五次提名。
日常生活中的急诊室剧集《皮特》是去年的新秀，斩获多项大奖，其中包括最佳剧集奖、诺亚·怀尔的最佳男主角奖和凯瑟琳·拉纳萨的最佳女配角奖。
它已经是一部深受喜爱的老牌节目，它占据了今年的表演类别。怀尔再次获得提名，拉纳萨也是如此。泰勒·迪尔登、菲奥娜·道里夫和塞皮德·莫阿菲也获得了提名，其中《皮特》占据了七名女配角中的四名。
他们的三位演员获得了最佳男配角提名：帕特里克·鲍尔、肖恩·海托西和杰兰·豪厄尔。
在艾美奖的主要竞争者——比如 2025 年最佳电视剧提名作品《遣散费》——往往会在两季之间休息数年的时代，《皮特》又回来了，这是其将经典网络趋势与现代流媒体声望相结合的改变游戏规则模式的一部分。《白莲花》在两季之间的缺席也帮助了它在表演类别上的地位，该剧的剧团也表现出了类似的统治力。
AppleTV+ 推出的两部新剧，即单人对抗集体思维的剧情片《Pluribus》和恐怖喜剧《寡妇湾》，在第一季中都取得了巨大的成功。
《绝命毒师》和《风骚律师》创作者文斯·吉利根的《Pluribus》获得了 18 项提名。他们将剧情类最佳女主角颁给了剧中唯一的主演李·西洪 (Rhee Seahorn)，她被认为是赢得该奖项的热门人选。
《寡妇湾》获得了 19 名，在喜剧片中仅次于《黑客》，其中明星马修·里斯获得最佳男主角奖。另外两部 AppleTV+ 节目获得了最佳喜剧奖，分别是《玛戈的钱有问题》和《收缩》，该剧的主演艾丽·范宁和米歇尔·菲佛也获得了表演提名。
近年来，苹果公司在顶级喜剧类别中取得了胜利，其中《泰德·拉索》和《工作室》大获全胜，去年《工作室》创下了 13 项艾美奖的纪录。今年它的缺席为《黑客》和《寡妇湾》留下了一个巨大的空缺。
《缩小》的提名包括哈里森·福特获得喜剧类最佳男配角提名。预测者表示，福特的力量非常强大，许多人表示，今年这位好莱坞传奇人物在经历了传奇的职业生涯后，终于赢得了 EGOT 级别的奖项。他的搭档杰森·席格尔因这部讲述治疗师跨越界限的剧获得了男主角提名。
Netflix 的《牛肉》以 16 项提名在限定剧或选集剧集类别中名列前茅。《牛肉》在 2023 年的第一季占据主导地位，该选集的全新恩怨持有者凯瑞·穆里根、奥斯卡·伊萨克和查尔斯·梅尔顿都获得了提名。
HBO Max 的三部剧集《镀金时代》获得最佳剧集最高奖提名；《七国骑士》和《皮特》。苹果公司也有自己的三个，分别是“Slow Horses”、“Your Friends & Neighbors”和“Pluribus”。
《幸福感》时隔多年重返艾美奖，并获得七项提名。赞达亚 (Zendaya) 在 2020 年和 2022 年的前两季获得剧情类最佳女主角，最近播出的第三季也获得了认可。
最近的获奖者丽莎·科隆-扎亚斯和杰夫·希勒在洛杉矶电视学院宣布了提名者。第 78 届黄金时段艾美奖颁奖典礼将在 NBC 播出，将于 9 月 14 日在孔雀剧院举行，这里是艾美奖的长期举办地，很快也将成为奥斯卡颁奖典礼的举办地。几十年来，玛莉丝卡·哈吉塔 (Mariska Hargitay) 一直是 NBC 的旗手之一，担任《法律与秩序：特殊受害者》的明星，她将主持纪录片《我的妈妈杰恩》，并获得导演和制作人的双重提名。

item.title: 电影评论：真人版《莫阿娜》是动画经典的无生命复制品
item.description:
不管你对它们有什么看法，但迪士尼真人翻拍至少给了我们一个选择。您更愿意看到哪一个？一部充满活力、翱翔天际的动画版《莫阿娜》，还是一部由道恩·强森和法比奥头发的无目的翻拍版？
海是广阔的，我的朋友们。然而，波利尼西亚旅行公主莫阿娜自从 10 年前首次出现在我们的屏幕上以来，似乎一直被困在游泳圈中。她是迪士尼最伟大的英雄之一。这部原版电影由手绘大师罗恩·克莱门茨和约翰·马斯克制作电脑动画，并在林-曼努尔·米兰达充满活力的歌曲的推动下，代表了本世纪迪士尼动画的高度。
但从那时起，水就变得汹涌澎湃。《海洋奇缘 2》最初计划作为一部流媒体剧集，但几乎没有对故事进行扩展。而新的真人翻拍版则做得更少。无论票房收入如何，很少有真人翻拍的影片能够获得荣耀。但由《汉密尔顿》老手托马斯·凯尔执导的《莫阿娜》感觉特别不合时宜，只不过是把卡通想象换成了真人版，可悲的是。死气沉沉的结果非常接近《周六夜现场》的戏仿或《蜘蛛侠宇宙》电影中那些开玩笑的平行宇宙之一。
新的《莫阿娜》通常是对原版进行逐个镜头、逐个音符的翻拍，其中加入了一些新材料。这在某种程度上是可以理解的。歌曲还是很棒的。扮演莫阿娜的凯瑟琳·拉加艾亚 (Catherine Laga'aia) 歌声优美。数字化的海洋更加蔚蓝。
但所有的创造力都已消失殆尽。动画的一大优点是它可以通过弹指完成任何事情。你可以感觉到想象力在发挥作用。原版《莫阿纳》的辉煌之处在于其丰富的波利尼西亚联系和卡通乐趣：半神毛伊（约翰逊饰）傻乎乎的变形，巨蟹塔马托亚（杰梅因·克莱门特饰）的脚趾轻敲动作，海洋将莫阿纳带回船上的方式。
让这些东西成为现实不仅仅是格式上的更新；它耗尽了他们活泼的灵魂。结果看起来更像是戏剧表演，而不是真正的艺术创作。我敢打赌，没有人在看过第一部《莫阿纳》中的小椰子海盗后对自己说：“我需要一个看起来更真实的卡卡莫拉。”
真人版更新的最大好处在于再现了莫阿娜的原生莫图努伊。如果说原版《莫阿娜》是为了向波利尼西亚文化致敬而制作的，那么真人的存在则让这一点变得更加切实。汤加血统的新西兰演员约翰·图伊 (John Tui) 尤其擅长饰演莫阿娜的首席父亲。
但从动画到真人的跨越往往会带来损失。对于注定要成为卡通的角色来说尤其如此，比如公鸡黑黑。塔马托亚的情况就是令人痛苦，他的“So Shiny”曲调曾经是不可否认的亮点，但现在可能是电影中最尴尬的场景。
这一切都不是拉加艾亚的错，她生动的表演是影片前进动力的主要来源。毛伊——自夸、狡猾的滑稽、不可避免的英雄——是约翰逊最适合的角色之一。因此，令人惊讶的是，他在这里的表演很难与动画毛伊岛的神韵相媲美。最近越来越多地被戏剧角色所吸引的约翰逊可能已经不再是一个原本是幻想的角色，而假发不足以掩盖这种差异。
随着克里斯托弗·诺兰的《奥德赛》即将登陆影院，对于有关诸神和归来的航海神话来说，这是一个异常好的时机。没有人知道这个系列会走多远——第三部动画电影正在开发中。但莫阿纳越来越迫切需要一些新的水域来探索。
华特迪士尼公司周四在影院上映的《莫阿娜》因动作/危险、一些恐怖的画面、粗鲁的幽默和简短的主题元素而被美国电影协会评为 PG 级。运行时间：115分钟。四颗星中的一颗半。

item.title: 特朗普表示与伊朗的停火“结束”后，油价上涨 5%，道指下跌 500 点
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——在唐纳德·特朗普总统对与伊朗的临时休战表示怀疑后，周三全球油价上涨，股市下跌。
在特朗普表示暂停战斗的协议已经“结束”后，标准普尔 500 指数下跌 0.3%，尽管他补充说他将允许谈判继续进行。截至下午 1 点 36 分，道琼斯工业平均指数下跌 562 点，即 1%。东部时间，纳斯达克综合指数上涨 0.1%，这得益于包括 Nvidia 在内的几只大型人工智能股票的上涨。
石油市场的走势更为强劲，每桶布伦特原油价格上涨 5.8%，至 78.43 美元。这远低于战争初期的峰值，当时交易最活跃的合约价格达到近 120 美元。但油价的上涨令人不安，因为油价刚刚回落至战前水平。
令人担忧的是，战争的持续将封锁霍尔木兹海峡，阻止从波斯湾向世界各地的客户运送原油。这可能会加剧通货膨胀，经济学家预计通货膨胀会随着油价上涨而缓解，进而迫使美联储和其他央行加息。
较高的利率可以抑制通货膨胀，但也会减缓经济发展并损害各种投资的价格。
在特朗普就停火状态表示“对我来说，我认为一切都结束了”后，欧洲股市加速下跌，油价立即攀升。他补充说，美国代表可以继续谈判，“但我认为他们在浪费时间。”
特朗普后来表示，美国正在为另一晚对伊朗发动袭击做准备。
在华尔街，燃油费用高的公司大幅下跌。美国航空下跌 3.3%，联合航空下跌 2.1%。
房地产行业公司的股票也疲软。他们担心债券市场的国债收益率上升将导致抵押贷款利率上升并令该行业降温，这让他们感到受伤。
销售台面、窗户和其他建筑用品的 Builders FirstSource 股价下跌 3.6%。住宅建筑商 PulteGroup 下跌 4.1%，D.R.霍顿下跌4%。
宣伟 (Sherwin-Williams) 股价下跌 3.4%，家得宝 (Home Depot) 下跌 2.5%，这是道琼斯指数走向一个月来最严重跌幅的两个最大原因。
人工智能行业一些有影响力的股票的稳定有助于抵消这些损失。最近几周，他们一直承受着压力，担心自己的价格过高，人工智能可能无法产生足够的生产力和利润，使芯片和数据中心的所有投资都值得。
它们的波动对华尔街影响很大，因为人工智能股票已经成长为美国市场最大的股票之一，这使得它们的走势对标准普尔 500 指数的影响比其他股票更大。
例如，英伟达股价上涨 3%，是推动标准普尔 500 指数上涨的最强劲力量，因为它是华尔街最大的股票。
另一个强劲的上涨推动力来自博通，该公司上涨 5.9%。苹果宣布与博通达成多年承诺，为其产品设计和生产定制组件。苹果表示，该协议的价值可能超过 300 亿美元。
在债券市场，国债收益率随着油价上涨而上涨。10 年期美国国债收益率从周二晚间的 4.55% 以及伊朗战争开始前的 3.97% 攀升至 4.58%。
海外股市方面，特朗普发表上述言论后，欧洲股市跌幅进一步扩大，德国DAX指数下跌2.2%。
在亚洲，韩国综合股价指数下跌 5.3%，并在对主导市场的人工智能股票的担忧和乐观情绪的双重影响下继续大幅波动。
香港恒生指数表现异常，上涨 3%。
中国人工智能初创公司智普（又名 Z.ai，交易名称为 Knowledge Atlas Technology）在香港交易的股票上涨 13.4%。
该公司一月份在香港上市后，“基石”投资者的六个月禁售期将于本周到期。中央人民广播电台周二晚间报道称，尽管此前担心禁售期到期可能引发抛售，但近 70% 的智浦基石投资者仍决心继续持有。
自上市以来，智浦股价已上涨逾1300%。
美联社商业作家 Matt Ott、Chan Ho-him 和 Elaine Kurtenbach 对本报告做出了贡献。

item.title: Edmunds 表示，价格低于 15,000 美元的最二手轿车
item.description:
根据Edmunds的数据，一辆3年二手车的平均交易价格超过3万美元。与购买新产品相比，这是一个很大的折扣，但对于很多人来说，它仍然可能在经济上遥不可及。幸运的是，花大约一半的钱仍然可以买到一辆兼具舒适性、可靠性、燃油经济性和现代技术的汽车。
Edmunds 专家已经确定了他们建议您重点关注的四款小型轿车和一款中型轿车。每辆车都具有具有竞争力的 Edmunds 评分、二手市场上的广泛可用性以及来自 RepairPal（数据驱动可靠性评级的独立来源）的至少 4 分（满分 5 分）的可靠性评分。我们还注意到您可以在 Carvana 和 CarMax 等全国零售商处找到符合我们 15,000 美元价格上限的最新车型。
思域作为小型轿车中长期最受欢迎的地位是当之无愧的。它提供出色的燃油经济性和强劲的加速性能，而其悬架调校则在乘坐质量和运动能力之间实现了近乎完美的平衡。尽管思域的中央触摸屏有时使用起来很慢而且烦人，但思域宽敞的后座和紧凑的结构给人留下了深刻的印象。
寻找：较旧的思域很容易落入我们的价格上限，但如果可以的话，目标是购买 2016 款思域。它今年进行了全面重新设计，具有新的造型、更宽敞的内部空间和新的可选涡轮增压发动机，可提供充足的动力和高英里/加仑。
起亚的 Forte 是该品牌的小型轿车，直到几年前才出售，后来起亚用 K4 取代了它。Forte 鲜明的造型、安静的内饰以及丰富的可用功能使其感觉像是一辆比其价格所暗示的更精致的小型轿车。它还具有良好的燃油经济性，并具有宽敞且易于装载的后备箱。Forte 的乘坐质量可能有点太硬，颠簸时不舒服，但总的来说，对于二手轿车来说，它是一个明智的选择。
寻找：最后一代 Forte 于 2019 车型年首次亮相。从今年到 2021 年左右的任何 Forte 都应该很容易以低于 15,000 美元的价格找到。在 Forte 的可用装饰级别中，EX 值得关注，因为它配备了高级功能，例如合成皮革内饰以及加热和通风前排座椅。
虽然这一组中的大多数轿车要么根本不运动，要么只是装饰得恰到好处，但马自达 3 确实具有驾驶乐趣。即使性能不是您的首要任务，这种响应能力在紧急演习期间也可能是宝贵的资产。马自达3锐利的外观设计也颇具风格，而其内饰质量超出预期，其碰撞测试成绩使其跻身同级车之首。
寻找：第三代马自达 3 于 2014 年首次亮相，并于 2018 年生产。买家应该不难从该生产的后期中找到可靠的选择。2018 Touring 车型提供了一系列符合价格的标准功能，并配备了比低配车型更强大的发动机。
丰田卡罗拉是紧凑型轿车类别中长期运行的主打车型，以其提供简洁的运输和较低的运行成本而闻名。卡罗拉因其舒适性和一系列吸引人的先进驾驶员辅助功能而获得高分。它不像一些竞争对手那么宽敞，而且缓慢的加速使乐趣因素相对较低，但除此之外，卡罗拉是一个明智的选择。
寻找：丰田在 2013 年推出了第 11 代 Corolla。您应该能够以低于 15,000 美元的价格购买 2015 至 2017 年的 Corolla。值得注意的是，2017 款 Corolla 的特点包括改进的前端造型、新的 LED 头灯和升级的内饰。
如果是本田雅阁或丰田凯美瑞等热门车型，找到一辆车龄不到 10 年且价格低于 15,000 美元的二手中型轿车可能会很困难。但这正是雪佛兰迈锐宝的吸引力所在。作为该组中唯一的中型轿车，马里布为乘客及其货物提供了比此列表中的其他轿车更多的空间，但它仍然提供经过验证的可靠性和强大的整体价值。
寻找：当前一代 Malibu 于 2016 年上市。今年或更新的任何 Malibu 都是不错的选择。如果您能找到一款，那么 2019 款 Malibu 值得一看，因为它经过修改的造型和更新的信息娱乐技术。
这些车型证明您不需要花大钱就能获得一辆可靠且装备精良的轿车。从风格和技术到乘坐质量和效率，真正的决定取决于对您来说最重要的事情。每一种都有一定的优势，但它们都物有所值。
这个故事是由汽车网站埃德蒙兹向美联社提供的。Bradley Iger 是 Edmunds 的撰稿人。
